greeting.speaker: Earl
greeting.text: Mornin'! Just the usual, if you don't mind. Black, no sugar.

thanks.speaker: Earl
thanks.text: You're a good kid, Cid. This town's lucky to have you.

rough_morning.speaker: Earl
rough_morning.text: Nah, not at all. Just like to get the day started right. Coffee, fresh air, friendly faces.

early.speaker: Earl
early.text: Always am. Old habit. Shop doesn't open till ten but I'm up at five regardless.

small_talk.speaker: Earl
small_talk.text: How long you been here now, Cid? Three years? Four?

four_years.speaker: Earl
four_years.text: Four years. Time flies. You ever think about moving on? Seeing more of the world?

choices.text: Sometimes. But I like it here.
Not really. This feels like home.
Funny you ask. Something keeps me here.

forever.speaker: Earl
forever.text: Ha. That's what most folks say. Something about this town just... settles into you.

no_track.speaker: Earl
no_track.text: Smart man. Live in the present. That's the way to do it.

likes_it.speaker: Earl
likes_it.text: Nothing wrong with that. Quiet life's an honest life.

home.speaker: Earl
home.text: That's good to hear. Real good.

something_keeps.speaker: Earl
something_keeps.text: Ha. Mountain air'll do that. Hard to leave once it gets in your lungs.

farewell.speaker: Earl
farewell.text: Well, I best get back before Mrs. Patterson shows up for her trim. You take care now, Cid.

greeting_return.speaker: Earl
greeting_return.text: Still holding down the fort I see. Good man.
